web_frameworks_detected.rust: axum 0.7 (hexagon/templates/rust/Cargo.toml:9, with tokio + tower-http + hyper + sqlx)

language_stack.workspace_kind: virtual Cargo workspace (root Cargo.toml members = []); buildable surfaces are HexaKit/ (submodule) and per-language libs/{rust,go,python,typescript} sub-workspaces

hygiene_evidence.LICENSE: LICENSE (1068B, MIT, Copyright 2026 Koosha Pari) + LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE
hygiene_evidence.README: README.md (7067B, 160 lines, work-state header pinned to MSRV/deny/audit, 12-category matrix, agent interaction matrix, dual-role clarification, quick-start)
hygiene_evidence.CHANGELOG: CHANGELOG.md (Keep-a-Changelog 1.1.0 format, Unreleased section + cliff.toml present for auto-gen)
hygiene_evidence.EDITORCONFIG: .editorconfig (446B, root=true, per-language indent_size 4 vs 2 for ts/js)
hygiene_evidence.CI: .github/workflows/ (15 workflows: ci, cargo-deny, cargo-audit, cargo-machete, cargo-semver-checks, codeql, docs-deploy, journey-gate, publish, quality-gate, scorecard, secrets-scan, trufflehog + 2 more)
hygiene_evidence.CONTRIBUTING: CONTRIBUTING.md (1095B, layer-contract-aware, semver policy) - NOTE: content is stale (still references 'template-commons' predecessor name) but file is substantive
hygiene_evidence.SECURITY: SECURITY.md (894B, GitHub Security Advisories preferred channel, 2-day ack / 5-day triage SLA, supported-versions table, repo security rules)
hygiene_evidence.FUNDING: FUNDING.yml (root, github: KooshaPari + custom sponsor URL) + .github/FUNDING.yml

dup_exposure: phenotype-request-id (request ID propagation - should be wired into all 3 web templates: axum middleware, chi middleware, FastAPI middleware)
phenotype-postfx (middleware runner / post-effect chain - natural fit for libs/go/phenotype-go-middleware chi chain and FastAPI Depends() chain)
phenotype-bus (event bus primitives - hexagon templates do not publish request lifecycle events; chi and fastapi templates are silent)
phenotype-registry (service registry - phenotype-go-kit/phenotype-py-kit could resolve service names into tracing/metrics labels automatically)
phenotype-dep-guard (org-wide deny.toml + audit-check config - PhenoKits' deny.toml could be a thin overlay on the dep-guard baseline)
phenotype-journeys (journey tracing primitives - could be emitted from the 3 web templates with zero per-stack code)
phenotype-shared / phenotype-tooling (shared just/lint/CI recipes - PhenoKits has its own justfile and Taskfile.yml; candidate for consolidation)
phenotype-hub (federated registry/CLI - could subsume the HexaKit template CLI surface)
phenotype-icons (UI asset library - could be consumed by docs/guides and any webapp template)
phenotype-omlx (OmniRoute MLX LLM bridge - could power an 'LLM-enabled phenotype-api' variant of templates/phenotype-api)
phenotype-ops-mcp (MCP tooling - could expose a 'scaffold new pheno project' tool that picks the right template from this monorepo)
sota_gaps: No shared cross-stack HTTP middleware contract - axum, chi, and FastAPI each define their own middleware composition with no shared trait/port; no phenotype-http-ports (Rust) or analogue in py/ts

No auto-instrumentation in any of the 3 web templates - tracing is declared in hexagon/templates/rust/Cargo.toml:30 but no #[instrument] macros or tower-http::trace middleware wired into example handlers; FastAPI has no structlog/timing middleware in phenotype-api; chi has no OTel middleware in phenotype-go-middleware
PII redaction / span field allow-list - none of the 3 web templates redact Authorization headers, query params, or body fields before logging
No tail-based or adaptive sampling guidance in any template - every template uses AlwaysOn or unspecified default
Exemplars (metric->trace correlation) - no metric instrumentation in any web template that would emit exemplars
No service-name resolution / registry bootstrap - FastAPI app factory and chi router both hard-code service names; phenotype-registry is unconsumed
No event-bus integration in any web template - request lifecycle events (start/finish/error) are not published to phenotype-bus in any of the 3 stacks
Dependabot only watches cargo and gomod ecosystems - libs/python (pip) and libs/typescript (npm) are unmonitored for upstream security updates; phenotype-py-kit pins fastapi>=0.128 with no auto-PR
CONTRIBUTING.md and COMPARISON.md are stale - both still reference 'template-commons', a predecessor identity that confuses new contributors
CHANGELOG.md is scaffold-only - 'Initial CHANGELOG scaffold' is the only entry; cliff.toml is configured but no auto-generated 0.1.0 release notes exist
No PII scrubbing / cardinality guard in any of the 3 web templates - high-cardinality path params flow straight into logs
No MCP server / registry bridge for the 12-category artifacts - agents cannot enumerate available templates/configs/libs at runtime; they have to read the README matrix
HexaKit submodule is the only 'real' buildable surface; root Cargo.toml has members=[] which makes cargo metadata report 'workspace has no members' - confusing for first-time contributors
convergence_rationale: Three independent web stacks (axum/Rust + chi/Go + fastapi/Python) coexist with no shared HTTP middleware trait, no shared request-id propagation, and no shared service-name registry bootstrap. The repo is the org's template + artifact monorepo, not a single web service, and the architectural intent across the 3 stacks is convergence (one observability/registry/bus contract) but the implementation is still stack-local. This matches the multi-stack pattern, with a clear convergence trajectory.

action_items.title: Wire phenotype-request-id and phenotype-postfx into all 3 web templates (axum + chi + fastapi)
action_items.why: Highest-leverage dup-exposure win. Each web template currently has its own (or absent) request-id middleware; centralizing eliminates ~150 lines of duplicated code and unlocks trace correlation across stacks.

action_items.title: Add pip and npm ecosystems to .github/dependabot.yml
action_items.why: Closes a real security gap - libs/python (fastapi, structlog) and libs/typescript (3 packages) currently have no automated dependency-update coverage. Dead-simple config change with high blast radius.

action_items.title: Author a phenotype-http-ports trait crate (Rust) + Python Protocol + TS interface to unify the 3 web stacks
action_items.why: The convergence play. A shared HttpMiddleware/RequestContext/ResponseContext port lets axum, chi (via go bindings), and FastAPI all consume the same observability/registry/bus primitives. Largest single convergence unlock.

action_items.title: Migrate CONTRIBUTING.md and COMPARISON.md off the stale 'template-commons' predecessor name
action_items.why: Both files still reference 'template-commons' which is the predecessor identity; new contributors hit a naming mismatch with the README. Trivial text edit but high signal-to-noise for repo polish.

action_items.title: Populate CHANGELOG.md with a real 0.1.0 release entry and add phenotype-registry service-name bootstrap to phenotype-api and phenotype-go-kit
action_items.why: Resolves the 'scaffold-only CHANGELOG' hygiene nit and lands the first concrete dup-exposure consumer of phenotype-registry, which unblocks metric label hygiene across all 3 web stacks.

metadata.workspace_crate_count: virtual (members=[]) + HexaKit submodule + 4 per-language libs sub-workspaces
